subject: Electrotherapy II
definition: Therapeutic Ultrasound utilizes high-frequency acoustic waves to produce thermal and non-thermal (mechanical) physiological effects in tissues.
clinicalPearls: The transducer head must be kept constantly moving to avoid hot spots and standing wave formation.
vivaQuestions: What is the piezoelectric effect in ultrasound transducer heads?

mcqs.question: Which frequency list represents standard therapeutic ultrasound?
mcqs.explanation: Therapeutic ultrasound typically uses high high frequencies of 1 MHz (for deeper tissues) and 3 MHz (for superficial tissues).
